entry.content: It was a great experience to volunteer at SAP ACC LAC, especially at the beginning of my SAP journey. I hope to be part of more events like this and continue enjoying great moments with such kind and dedicated people who inspire me. Thank you all!

entry.title: Automating SAP HANA System Replication on AWS with Terraform and Ansible
entry.content:
Introduction

This is a follow-up to my earlier post on deploying SAP HANA 2.0 on AWS with Terraform and Ansible. That covered a single-node installation. In this walkthrough, we go from nothing to active replication in 21 mins. 

SAP HANA System Replication (HSR) paid my mortgage for about 12 / 13 years, but I don't get to play with it much anymore, which is a shame. This post automates the whole setup — two EC2 instances in separate availability zones, HANA installed on both, HSR configured end to end with no manual steps.

I might follow it up with a blog about HA, but I don't believe that Pacemaker / STONITH is fit for purpose. On nearly every project that I've worked on that had Pacemaker, we ended up tearing it out in favour of standard HSR with manual failover. We'll see.

Everything here was built and tested live. The gotchas section reflects what actually broke.

Note:  The HANA data (50GB), log (25GB) and shared (50GB) filesystems each have their own dedicated EBS volumes, so the root volume only needs to accommodate the OS and the HANA installation media (~15GB). 50GB is sufficient. Remember this is a sandbox. Use proper SAP Sizing tools for productive use.

Architecture

Local machine
    ├── Terraform  → VPC, two subnets (AZ-a, AZ-b), IGW, Route 53 private zone
    │               two EC2 instances, IAM role for S3 access
    │
    └── Ansible    → hana-install.yml  (runs on both nodes in parallel)
                     hsr-setup.yml    (primary first, then secondary, then verify)

AWS VPC (10.0.0.0/16)
    ├── Subnet 10.0.1.0/24 (us-east-2a)  →  hana-primary   (r5.xlarge)
    └── Subnet 10.0.2.0/24 (us-east-2b)  →  hana-secondary (r5.xlarge)

Replication: SYNCMEM, logreplay mode

The security group allows all inbound traffic within the VPC (10.0.0.0/16) so HSR can communicate freely between nodes. HANA System Replication uses ports in the 4000x and 5432x ranges depending on instance number — rather than enumerate them, open the VPC CIDR internally and restrict SSH at the perimeter.

Project Layout

ansible_hsr/
├── terraform/
│   ├── providers.tf
│   ├── variables.tf
│   ├── main.tf
│   └── outputs.tf
├── ansible/
│   ├── inventory.ini
│   ├── hana-install.yml
│   ├── hsr-setup.yml
│   └── templates/
│       └── hana_install.cfg.j2
└── generate_inventory.sh

Step 1 — Terraform

What it provisions

  • VPC with DNS support and hostnames enabled (required for HANA internal resolution)
  • Two subnets in separate AZs for HA placement
  • Internet gateway and route table
  • Route 53 private hosted zone (hana.internal) with A records for both nodes
  • IAM role + policy granting S3 read access to the HANA media bucket — no credentials on the instances
  • Security group: SSH from anywhere, all internal VPC traffic, all egress
  • Two EC2 instances: r5.xlarge, SUSE SLES for SAP 15 SP5 PAYG AMI

variables.tf

variable "aws_region" {
  default = "us-east-2"
}

variable "key_pair_name" {
  default = "<your-key-pair-name>"
}

variable "hana_media_bucket" {
  default = "<your-s3-bucket-name>"
}

variable "private_zone_name" {
  default = "hana.internal"
}

main.tf 

# SUSE SLES for SAP 15 SP5 PAYG (us-east-2)
locals {
  sles_sap_ami = "ami-099afc29551c4b139"
}

# IAM role so the instance can pull HANA media from S3
resource "aws_iam_role" "hana_s3" {
  name = "hana-s3-role"

  assume_role_policy = jsonencode({
    Version = "2012-10-17"
    Statement = [{
      Effect    = "Allow"
      Principal = { Service = "ec2.amazonaws.com" }
      Action    = "sts:AssumeRole"
    }]
  })
}

resource "aws_iam_role_policy" "hana_s3" {
  name = "hana-s3-read"
  role = aws_iam_role.hana_s3.id

  policy = jsonencode({
    Version = "2012-10-17"
    Statement = [{
      Effect   = "Allow"
      Action   = ["s3:GetObject", "s3:ListBucket"]
      Resource = [
        "arn:aws:s3:::${var.hana_media_bucket}",
        "arn:aws:s3:::${var.hana_media_bucket}/*"
      ]
    }]
  })
}

resource "aws_iam_instance_profile" "hana_s3" {
  name = "hana-s3-profile"
  role = aws_iam_role.hana_s3.name
}

resource "aws_security_group" "firewalld_test" {
  name        = "firewalld-test-sg"
  description = "SSH open - firewalld testing instance"

  ingress {
    description = "SSH from anywhere"
    from_port   = 22
    to_port     = 22
    protocol    = "tcp"
    cidr_blocks = ["0.0.0.0/0"]
  }

  egress {
    from_port   = 0
    to_port     = 0
    protocol    = "-1"
    cidr_blocks = ["0.0.0.0/0"]
  }

  tags = {
    Name = "firewalld-test-sg"
  }
}

resource "aws_instance" "hana" {
  ami                    = local.sles_sap_ami
  instance_type          = "r5.xlarge"
  key_name               = var.key_pair_name
  vpc_security_group_ids = [aws_security_group.firewalld_test.id]
  iam_instance_profile   = aws_iam_instance_profile.hana_s3.name

  root_block_device {
    volume_type = "gp3"
    volume_size = 50
  }

  tags = {
    Name = "hana-server"
  }
}

resource "aws_ebs_volume" "hana_data" {
  availability_zone = aws_instance.hana.availability_zone
  size              = 50
  type              = "gp3"
  tags              = { Name = "hana-data" }
}

resource "aws_ebs_volume" "hana_log" {
  availability_zone = aws_instance.hana.availability_zone
  size              = 25
  type              = "gp3"
  tags              = { Name = "hana-log" }
}

resource "aws_ebs_volume" "hana_shared" {
  availability_zone = aws_instance.hana.availability_zone
  size              = 50
  type              = "gp3"
  tags              = { Name = "hana-shared" }
}

resource "aws_volume_attachment" "hana_data" {
  device_name = "/dev/sdf"
  volume_id   = aws_ebs_volume.hana_data.id
  instance_id = aws_instance.hana.id
}

resource "aws_volume_attachment" "hana_log" {
  device_name = "/dev/sdg"
  volume_id   = aws_ebs_volume.hana_log.id
  instance_id = aws_instance.hana.id
}

resource "aws_volume_attachment" "hana_shared" {
  device_name = "/dev/sdh"
  volume_id   = aws_ebs_volume.hana_shared.id
  instance_id = aws_instance.hana.id
}

outputs.tf

output "primary_public_ip"    { value = aws_instance.hana_primary.public_ip }
output "secondary_public_ip"  { value = aws_instance.hana_secondary.public_ip }
output "primary_private_ip"   { value = aws_instance.hana_primary.private_ip }
output "secondary_private_ip" { value = aws_instance.hana_secondary.private_ip }

Run (this section took less than a minute)

cd terraform
terraform init
terraform apply -auto-approve

Step 2 — Generate the Ansible Inventory

After terraform apply, run generate_inventory.sh from the repo root. It reads the Terraform outputs, validates the IPs, constructs the primary's private hostname, and writes ansible/inventory.ini.

#!/bin/bash
SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)"
cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/terraform"

PRIMARY_PUBLIC=$(terraform output -raw primary_public_ip 2>&1)
SECONDARY_PUBLIC=$(terraform output -raw secondary_public_ip 2>&1)
PRIMARY_PRIVATE=$(terraform output -raw primary_private_ip 2>&1)

if [[ ! "$PRIMARY_PUBLIC" =~ ^[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+$ || \
      ! "$SECONDARY_PUBLIC" =~ ^[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+$ ]]; then
  echo "ERROR: No valid IPs from Terraform — run terraform apply first"
  exit 1
fi

PRIMARY_HOSTNAME="ip-$(echo "$PRIMARY_PRIVATE" | tr '.' '-')"

cat > "$SCRIPT_DIR/ansible/inventory.ini" <<EOF
[primary]
hana-primary ansible_host=${PRIMARY_PUBLIC} ansible_user=ec2-user ansible_ssh_private_key_file=~/.ssh/HANA_DEP.pem

[secondary]
hana-secondary ansible_host=${SECONDARY_PUBLIC} ansible_user=ec2-user ansible_ssh_private_key_file=~/.ssh/HANA_DEP.pem

[hana:children]
primary
secondary

[hana:vars]
ansible_ssh_common_args='-o StrictHostKeyChecking=no -o UserKnownHostsFile=/dev/null -o ServerAliveInterval=30 -o ServerAliveCountMax=20'
primary_private_hostname=${PRIMARY_HOSTNAME}
EOF

The primary_private_hostname variable is critical — see the gotchas section.

./generate_inventory.sh

Step 3 — Install HANA on Both Nodes (this section took 16 mins)

hana-install.yml (Appendix A) runs against [hana] (both nodes in parallel). It handles all prerequisites — packages, kernel parameters, swap, ulimits, THP, saptune — downloads the HANA media from S3, and runs hdblcm in silent/batch mode.

Key points:

  • The IAM instance profile gives each node S3 read access — AWS CLI uses it automatically, no credentials needed on the instance
  • hdblcm is run with async: 3600, poll: 30 — installation takes 20–40 minutes and will time out a normal SSH connection
  • A stat check prevents hdblcm re-running if HANA is already installed
  • The backup directory /hana/backup is chowned to hdbadm:sapsys after hdblcm runs — hdbadm doesn't exist until HANA installs
cd ansible
ansible-playbook -i inventory.ini hana-install.yml

Step 4 — Configure HSR (4 minutes)

hsr-setup.yml (Appendix B) is three plays:

  1. Primary — take backups, enable HSR, copy PKI files to control node
  2. Secondary — stop HANA, push PKI files, register with primary, start HANA
  3. Primary — verify replication is ACTIVE
ansible-playbook -i inventory.ini hsr-setup.yml

The full playbook is in the appendix. The gotchas below explain every decision in it.

Gotchas

1. HANA MDC requires backups of BOTH databases before sr_enable

HANA 2.0 runs as Multi-Database Container (MDC) by default. There is a SYSTEMDB and one or more tenant databases (in this case HDB). hdbnsutil -sr_enable will refuse to run unless both have a complete data backup:

Backup has not yet been executed for database 'HDB'
Primary needs initial data backup for system replication!

A SYSTEMDB backup alone is not enough. The playbook runs both explicitly:

- name: Run SYSTEMDB backup
  shell: |
    su - hdbadm -c "
      hdbsql -i 00 -d SYSTEMDB -u SYSTEM -p 'Hana1234!' \
      \"BACKUP DATA USING FILE ('systemdb_backup')\"
    "
  changed_when: true

- name: Run tenant database backup
  shell: |
    su - hdbadm -c "
      hdbsql -i 00 -d HDB -u SYSTEM -p 'Hana1234!' \
      \"BACKUP DATA USING FILE ('tenant_backup')\"
    "
  changed_when: true

Then waits for each to appear in the backup catalog before proceeding:

- name: Wait for SYSTEMDB backup in catalog
  shell: |
    su - hdbadm -c "
      hdbsql -i 00 -d SYSTEMDB -u SYSTEM -p 'Hana1234!' \
      \"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM SYS.M_BACKUP_CATALOG
        WHERE ENTRY_TYPE_NAME='complete data backup'
        AND STATE_NAME='successful'\"
    "
  register: systemdb_backup_catalog
  until: (systemdb_backup_catalog.stdout | regex_search('[1-9][0-9]*')) is not none
  retries: 20
  delay: 15
  changed_when: false

Gotcha: regex_search() in Ansible returns a string (the match) or None — not a boolean. Using it bare in until: raises a "Conditional result was derived from value of type str" error. The is not none cast makes it a proper boolean.

2. PKI files 

HANA HSR uses PKI/SSFS files to authenticate replication between nodes. These must be copied from the primary to the secondary so both nodes trust each other. The files are:

/usr/sap/HDB/SYS/global/security/rsecssfs/data/SSFS_HDB.DAT
/usr/sap/HDB/SYS/global/security/rsecssfs/key/SSFS_HDB.KEY

The playbook copies them to /tmp on the primary, fetches them to the control node with Ansible's fetch module, then pushes them to the secondary with copy:

- name: Copy PKI files to tmp
  shell: |
    cp /usr/sap/HDB/SYS/global/security/rsecssfs/data/SSFS_HDB.DAT /tmp/SSFS_HDB.DAT
    cp /usr/sap/HDB/SYS/global/security/rsecssfs/key/SSFS_HDB.KEY /tmp/SSFS_HDB.KEY
    chmod 644 /tmp/SSFS_HDB.DAT /tmp/SSFS_HDB.KEY
  changed_when: true

- name: Fetch PKI DAT to control node
  fetch:
    src: "/tmp/SSFS_HDB.DAT"
    dest: "/tmp/SSFS_HDB.DAT"
    flat: true

On the secondary:

- name: Push PKI DAT file to secondary
  copy:
    src: "/tmp/SSFS_HDB.DAT"
    dest: "/usr/sap/HDB/SYS/global/security/rsecssfs/data/SSFS_HDB.DAT"
    owner: hdbadm
    group: sapsys
    mode: "0600"

3. Use the EC2 private hostname, not Route 53

When registering the secondary with the primary, hdbnsutil -sr_register --remoteHost= must match the hostname that HANA itself registered under. HANA on AWS registers using the EC2 internal hostname, which is always derived from the private IP:

10.0.1.154  →  ip-10-0-1-154

Route 53 names like hana-primary.hana.internal will not match and the registration will fail with an SSL/PKI error that looks like a certificate problem but is actually a hostname mismatch.

generate_inventory.sh constructs the correct hostname from the Terraform private IP output and writes it to [hana:vars] in inventory.ini:

PRIMARY_HOSTNAME="ip-$(echo "$PRIMARY_PRIVATE" | tr '.' '-')"

The secondary play reads it as {{ primary_private_hostname }} — no hardcoding.

4. hdbadm doesn't exist until HANA installs

The first time hana-install.yml runs, hdbadm and the sapsys group don't exist yet — they're created by hdblcm. Any task that references them (like setting ownership on /hana/backup) must run after the hdblcm task, not before.

5. Variables don't cross plays

Ansible plays are isolated. A variable set in the primary play is not available in the secondary play. This is why primary_private_hostname is in inventory.ini under [hana:vars] rather than set in the primary play and referenced later. Inventory group vars are available to all hosts in all plays.

Verifying Replication

Once the playbook completes, SSH to the primary and run:

ssh -i ~/.ssh/<your-key-pair-name>.pem ec2-user@<primary-public-ip>
sudo su - hdbadm
python3 /usr/sap/HDB/HDB00/exe/python_support/systemReplicationStatus.py

A healthy output looks like:

|Database |Host          |Port  |Service Name |Site ID |Site Name |Secondary     |Replication |Status |Fully Synced |
|SYSTEMDB |ip-10-0-1-154 |30001 |nameserver   |      1 |SITE1     |ip-10-0-2-150 |SYNCMEM     |ACTIVE |        True |
|HDB      |ip-10-0-1-154 |30007 |xsengine     |      1 |SITE1     |ip-10-0-2-150 |SYNCMEM     |ACTIVE |        True |
|HDB      |ip-10-0-1-154 |30003 |indexserver  |      1 |SITE1     |ip-10-0-2-150 |SYNCMEM     |ACTIVE |        True |

overall system replication status: ACTIVE

All three services — nameserver, xsengine, indexserver — must show ACTIVE and Fully Synced: True.

Tear Down

cd terraform
terraform destroy -auto-approve

The S3 bucket and HANA media are unmanaged by Terraform and persist between runs — you only pay S3 storage rates between deployments.

Key Lessons

Problem Root Cause Fix

sr_enable: backup not executedMDC has SYSTEMDB + tenant — both need backupsExplicit backup task for each DB with catalog wait
sr_register: SSL/PKI errorHostname mismatch — Route 53 name vs EC2 hostnameUse ip-<private-ip> format, derived from Terraform output
PKI copy failingWrong file pathFiles are at /usr/sap/HDB/SYS/global/security/rsecssfs/
hdbadm not foundRunning chown before hdblcm creates the userMove ownership task to after hdblcm
Backup catalog check errorregex_search() returns str not boolUse is not none to cast to boolean
HANA re-installs on re-runNo idempotency checkstat check on hdbnsutil before running hdblcm

Appendix

A) hana-install.yml

---
- name: SAP HANA prerequisites and installation
  hosts: all
  become: true
  gather_facts: true

  vars:
    hana_sid: "HDB"
    hana_instance: "00"
    hana_master_pw: "<your-password>"
    sapadm_password: "<your-password>"
    lss_password: "<your-password>"
    hana_s3_bucket: "<your-s3-bucket-name>"
    hana_s3_prefix: "SAP_HANA_DATABASE/DATA_UNITS/HDB_SERVER_LINUX_X86_64"
    hana_media_dir: "/hana/media"
    hana_install_dir: "/hana/shared"
    hana_data_dir: "/hana/data"
    hana_log_dir: "/hana/log"
    swap_size_mb: 20480

  tasks:

    - name: Confirm target is SUSE
      fail:
        msg: "This playbook requires SUSE Linux Enterprise Server. Got: {{ ansible_os_family }}"
      when: ansible_os_family != 'Suse'

    - name: Refresh zypper repositories
      zypper_repository:
        repo: '*'
        runrefresh: true
      ignore_errors: true

    - name: Install required OS packages
      zypper:
        name:
          - libaio1
          - libaio-devel
          - libltdl7
          - libatomic1
          - libnuma1
          - numactl
          - uuidd
          - rsync
          - lsof
          - unzip
          - xfsprogs
          - glibc-i18ndata
          - graphviz
          - expect
          - python3
        state: present

    - name: Enable and start uuidd
      systemd:
        name: uuidd
        state: started
        enabled: true

    - name: Check existing swap
      command: swapon --show=SIZE --noheadings --bytes
      register: swap_check
      changed_when: false
      failed_when: false

    - name: Create swapfile
      command: dd if=/dev/zero of=/swapfile bs=1M count={{ swap_size_mb }}
      when: swap_check.stdout == ""

    - name: Set swapfile permissions
      file:
        path: /swapfile
        mode: "0600"
      when: swap_check.stdout == ""

    - name: Format swapfile
      command: mkswap /swapfile
      when: swap_check.stdout == ""

    - name: Activate swapfile
      command: swapon /swapfile
      when: swap_check.stdout == ""

    - name: Persist swapfile in fstab
      lineinfile:
        path: /etc/fstab
        line: "/swapfile swap swap defaults 0 0"
        state: present

    - name: Set kernel parameters for HANA
      sysctl:
        name: "{{ item.key }}"
        value: "{{ item.value }}"
        sysctl_set: true
        state: present
        reload: true
      loop:
        - { key: "vm.max_map_count",            value: "2147483647" }
        - { key: "kernel.sem",                   value: "1250 256000 100 8192" }
        - { key: "net.core.somaxconn",           value: "4096" }
        - { key: "net.ipv4.tcp_max_syn_backlog", value: "8192" }
        - { key: "kernel.numa_balancing",        value: "0" }

    - name: Set ulimits for SAP
      copy:
        dest: /etc/security/limits.d/99-sap.conf
        mode: "0644"
        content: |
          @sapsys  soft  nofile  1048576
          @sapsys  hard  nofile  1048576
          @sapsys  soft  nproc   unlimited
          @sapsys  hard  nproc   unlimited
          @sdba    soft  nofile  1048576
          @sdba    hard  nofile  1048576

    - name: Disable THP at runtime
      shell: |
        echo never > /sys/kernel/mm/transparent_hugepage/enabled
        echo never > /sys/kernel/mm/transparent_hugepage/defrag
      changed_when: true

    - name: Create systemd unit to disable THP on boot
      copy:
        dest: /etc/systemd/system/disable-thp.service
        mode: "0644"
        content: |
          [Unit]
          Description=Disable Transparent Huge Pages
          After=network.target
          [Service]
          Type=oneshot
          ExecStart=/bin/sh -c "echo never > /sys/kernel/mm/transparent_hugepage/enabled && echo never > /sys/kernel/mm/transparent_hugepage/defrag"
          RemainAfterExit=yes
          [Install]
          WantedBy=multi-user.target

    - name: Enable disable-thp service
      systemd:
        name: disable-thp
        enabled: true
        daemon_reload: true

    - name: Create HANA filesystem directories
      file:
        path: "{{ item }}"
        state: directory
        mode: "0755"
      loop:
        - "{{ hana_install_dir }}"
        - "{{ hana_data_dir }}"
        - "{{ hana_log_dir }}"
        - "/hana/backup"

    - name: Install saptune
      zypper:
        name: saptune
        state: present
      ignore_errors: true

    - name: Apply SAP HANA tuning profile via saptune
      command: saptune solution apply HANA
      changed_when: true
      ignore_errors: true

    - name: Enable saptune daemon
      systemd:
        name: saptune
        enabled: true
        state: started
      ignore_errors: true

    - name: Check if aws cli is present
      command: which aws
      register: aws_cli_check
      changed_when: false
      failed_when: false

    - name: Download and install AWS CLI v2
      shell: |
        curl -fsSL "https://awscli.amazonaws.com/awscli-exe-linux-x86_64.zip" -o /tmp/awscliv2.zip
        unzip -q /tmp/awscliv2.zip -d /tmp/awscli-install
        /tmp/awscli-install/aws/install --bin-dir /usr/local/bin --install-dir /usr/local/aws-cli --update
        rm -rf /tmp/awscliv2.zip /tmp/awscli-install
      when: aws_cli_check.rc != 0

    - name: Sync HANA media from S3
      command: >
        aws s3 sync s3://{{ hana_s3_bucket }}/{{ hana_s3_prefix }}/ {{ hana_media_dir }}/
        --no-progress
      register: s3_sync
      changed_when: "'download' in s3_sync.stdout"

    - name: Find hdblcm installer
      find:
        paths: "{{ hana_media_dir }}"
        patterns: "hdblcm"
        recurse: true
      register: hdblcm_find

    - name: Fail if hdblcm not found
      fail:
        msg: "hdblcm not found under {{ hana_media_dir }} — check hana_s3_bucket and hana_s3_prefix"
      when: hdblcm_find.files | length == 0

    - name: Set hdblcm path fact
      set_fact:
        hdblcm_path: "{{ hdblcm_find.files[0].path }}"

    - name: Make hdblcm executable
      file:
        path: "{{ hdblcm_path }}"
        mode: "0755"

    - name: Write hdblcm silent response file
      template:
        src: hana_install.cfg.j2
        dest: /tmp/hana_install.cfg
        mode: "0600"
        force: true

    - name: Move LSS media aside
      command: mv {{ hana_media_dir }}/lss {{ hana_media_dir }}/lss_aside
      args:
        removes: "{{ hana_media_dir }}/lss"

    - name: Make media directory fully executable
      shell: chmod -R 755 {{ hana_media_dir }}
      changed_when: true

    - name: Check if HANA already installed
      stat:
        path: "/usr/sap/{{ hana_sid }}/HDB{{ hana_instance }}/exe/hdbnsutil"
      register: hana_installed

    - name: Run SAP HANA installation (hdblcm)
      command: >
        {{ hdblcm_path }} --batch --configfile=/tmp/hana_install.cfg
      register: hdblcm_result
      changed_when: true
      async: 3600
      poll: 30
      when: not hana_installed.stat.exists

    - name: Show hdblcm output
      debug:
        msg: "{{ hdblcm_result.stdout_lines | default(['HANA already installed, skipped']) }}"

    - name: Set backup directory ownership for hdbadm
      file:
        path: /hana/backup
        owner: hdbadm
        group: sapsys
        recurse: true

    - name: Check HDB daemon is running
      shell: |
        su - {{ hana_sid | lower }}adm -c "HDB info" 2>&1 | grep -E "hdbdaemon|hdbnameserver"
      register: hdb_info
      changed_when: false
      failed_when: hdb_info.rc != 0

    - name: Confirm HANA installation complete
      debug:
        msg: >
          SAP HANA {{ hana_sid }} instance {{ hana_instance }} installed successfully.
          Connect: hdbsql -i {{ hana_instance }} -u SYSTEM -p <password>

B) hsr-setup.yml

---
- name: Enable HSR on primary
  hosts: primary
  become: true
  gather_facts: true

  vars:
    hana_sid: "HDB"
    hana_instance: "00"
    hana_master_pw: "<your-password>"
    hsr_replication_mode: "syncmem"
    hsr_operation_mode: "logreplay"
    hsr_site_name_primary: "SITE1"

  tasks:

    - name: Run SYSTEMDB backup
      shell: |
        su - {{ hana_sid | lower }}adm -c "
          hdbsql -i {{ hana_instance }} -d SYSTEMDB -u SYSTEM -p '{{ hana_master_pw }}' \
          \"BACKUP DATA USING FILE ('systemdb_backup')\"
        "
      changed_when: true

    - name: Run tenant database backup
      shell: |
        su - {{ hana_sid | lower }}adm -c "
          hdbsql -i {{ hana_instance }} -d {{ hana_sid }} -u SYSTEM -p '{{ hana_master_pw }}' \
          \"BACKUP DATA USING FILE ('tenant_backup')\"
        "
      changed_when: true

    - name: Wait for SYSTEMDB backup in catalog
      shell: |
        su - {{ hana_sid | lower }}adm -c "
          hdbsql -i {{ hana_instance }} -d SYSTEMDB -u SYSTEM -p '{{ hana_master_pw }}' \
          \"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM SYS.M_BACKUP_CATALOG WHERE ENTRY_TYPE_NAME='complete data backup' AND STATE_NAME='successful'\"
        "
      register: systemdb_backup_catalog
      until: (systemdb_backup_catalog.stdout | regex_search('[1-9][0-9]*')) is not none
      retries: 20
      delay: 15
      changed_when: false

    - name: Wait for tenant backup in catalog
      shell: |
        su - {{ hana_sid | lower }}adm -c "
          hdbsql -i {{ hana_instance }} -d {{ hana_sid }} -u SYSTEM -p '{{ hana_master_pw }}' \
          \"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM SYS.M_BACKUP_CATALOG WHERE ENTRY_TYPE_NAME='complete data backup' AND STATE_NAME='successful'\"
        "
      register: tenant_backup_catalog
      until: (tenant_backup_catalog.stdout | regex_search('[1-9][0-9]*')) is not none
      retries: 20
      delay: 15
      changed_when: false

    - name: Check if HSR already enabled on primary
      shell: |
        su - {{ hana_sid | lower }}adm -c "hdbnsutil -sr_state"
      register: sr_state_check
      changed_when: false

    - name: Enable system replication on primary
      shell: |
        su - {{ hana_sid | lower }}adm -c "
          hdbnsutil -sr_enable --name={{ hsr_site_name_primary }}
        "
      changed_when: true
      when: "'mode: primary' not in sr_state_check.stdout"
      ignore_errors: true

    - name: Confirm primary HSR state
      shell: |
        su - {{ hana_sid | lower }}adm -c "hdbnsutil -sr_state"
      register: sr_state_primary
      changed_when: false

    - name: Show primary HSR state
      debug:
        msg: "{{ sr_state_primary.stdout_lines }}"

    - name: Fail if primary not in HSR primary mode
      fail:
        msg: "Primary HSR enable failed"
      when: "'mode: primary' not in sr_state_primary.stdout"

    - name: Copy PKI files to tmp
      shell: |
        cp /usr/sap/{{ hana_sid }}/SYS/global/security/rsecssfs/data/SSFS_{{ hana_sid }}.DAT /tmp/SSFS_{{ hana_sid }}.DAT
        cp /usr/sap/{{ hana_sid }}/SYS/global/security/rsecssfs/key/SSFS_{{ hana_sid }}.KEY /tmp/SSFS_{{ hana_sid }}.KEY
        chmod 644 /tmp/SSFS_{{ hana_sid }}.DAT /tmp/SSFS_{{ hana_sid }}.KEY
      changed_when: true

    - name: Fetch PKI DAT to control node
      fetch:
        src: "/tmp/SSFS_{{ hana_sid }}.DAT"
        dest: "/tmp/SSFS_{{ hana_sid }}.DAT"
        flat: true

    - name: Fetch PKI KEY to control node
      fetch:
        src: "/tmp/SSFS_{{ hana_sid }}.KEY"
        dest: "/tmp/SSFS_{{ hana_sid }}.KEY"
        flat: true

- name: Stop HANA on secondary and push PKI files
  hosts: secondary
  become: true
  gather_facts: true

  vars:
    hana_sid: "HDB"
    hana_instance: "00"
    hana_master_pw: "<your-password>"
    hsr_replication_mode: "syncmem"
    hsr_operation_mode: "logreplay"
    hsr_site_name_secondary: "SITE2"

  tasks:

    - name: Stop HANA on secondary
      shell: |
        su - {{ hana_sid | lower }}adm -c "HDB stop"
      changed_when: true

    - name: Wait for HANA to fully stop
      shell: |
        su - {{ hana_sid | lower }}adm -c "HDB info" 2>&1 | grep -c hdbdaemon || true
      register: stop_check
      until: stop_check.stdout | int == 0
      retries: 20
      delay: 15
      changed_when: false

    - name: Push PKI DAT file to secondary
      copy:
        src: "/tmp/SSFS_{{ hana_sid }}.DAT"
        dest: "/usr/sap/{{ hana_sid }}/SYS/global/security/rsecssfs/data/SSFS_{{ hana_sid }}.DAT"
        owner: hdbadm
        group: sapsys
        mode: "0600"

    - name: Push PKI KEY file to secondary
      copy:
        src: "/tmp/SSFS_{{ hana_sid }}.KEY"
        dest: "/usr/sap/{{ hana_sid }}/SYS/global/security/rsecssfs/key/SSFS_{{ hana_sid }}.KEY"
        owner: hdbadm
        group: sapsys
        mode: "0600"

    - name: Register secondary with primary
      shell: |
        su - {{ hana_sid | lower }}adm -c "
          hdbnsutil -sr_register \
            --name={{ hsr_site_name_secondary }} \
            --remoteHost={{ primary_private_hostname }} \
            --remoteInstance={{ hana_instance }} \
            --replicationMode={{ hsr_replication_mode }} \
            --operationMode={{ hsr_operation_mode }}
        "
      register: sr_register
      changed_when: true

    - name: Show registration output
      debug:
        msg: "{{ sr_register.stdout_lines }}"

    - name: Start HANA on secondary
      shell: |
        su - {{ hana_sid | lower }}adm -c "HDB start"
      changed_when: true
      async: 600
      poll: 30

- name: Verify replication state from primary
  hosts: primary
  become: true
  gather_facts: false

  vars:
    hana_sid: "HDB"
    hana_instance: "00"

  tasks:

    - name: Wait for secondary to connect
      shell: |
        su - {{ hana_sid | lower }}adm -c "
          python3 /usr/sap/{{ hana_sid }}/{{ hana_sid }}{{ hana_instance }}/exe/python_support/systemReplicationStatus.py
        " 2>&1 || true
      register: hsr_status
      until: "'ACTIVE' in hsr_status.stdout or 'SYNCING' in hsr_status.stdout"
      retries: 20
      delay: 15
      changed_when: false

    - name: Show replication status
      debug:
        msg: "{{ hsr_status.stdout_lines }}"

    - name: Confirm replication is active
      fail:
        msg: "HSR did not reach ACTIVE or SYNCING state"
      when: "'ACTIVE' not in hsr_status.stdout and 'SYNCING' not in hsr_status.stdout"

    - name: Final replication status
      shell: |
        su - {{ hana_sid | lower }}adm -c "
          python3 /usr/sap/{{ hana_sid }}/{{ hana_sid }}{{ hana_instance }}/exe/python_support/systemReplicationStatus.py
        "
      register: final_status
      changed_when: false
      ignore_errors: true

    - name: Show final replication status
      debug:
        msg: "{{ final_status.stdout_lines }}"

entry.title: Re: Hello SAP Community! My name is Veera Venkata Satyanarayana, an Aspiring Mechanical Engineer Stu
entry.content:
Thank you so much @David_Chaviano1  for asking !

Along with my 3 friends, I participated in a hardware hackathon where the components and problem statement were revealed on the same day. The problem was about reducing delays in communication between patient rooms and doctor/pharmacy stations in hospitals.

We proposed an Autonomous Patient Request Receiving Robot using Arduino. It was a line-following robot using IR sensors, ultrasonic sensor for obstacle detection, and L298 motor driver. Our actual thought was to detect patient requests using another IR sensor when the robot stopped in front of each room, but implementing it within the limited time became challenging. So, we modified the idea by using a light indication system from the patient side. If the light was ON, the robot will record the request and later reported it at the doctor station.

While testing, all components worked well individually and even after integration initially. But suddenly the Arduino got excess voltage and short-circuited. We replaced it instantly, but due to time limits we could not present a fully working prototype to the judges. Even though we felt disappointed, we learned a lot about teamwork, pressure handling, and real-time problem solving all within just 8 hours.

Later, I also thought about improving the idea further using a Bluetooth module so that emergency requests could instantly reach the monitoring station without waiting for the robot to complete all rooms.

entry.title: Common errors when accessing Learning via BizX
entry.content:
After Learning is connected to BizX and ready to be implemented and managed by Administrators or accessed by Users to complete their journeys, different navigation errors may be seen.

To help identify possible causes and solutions, the list below outlines the relevant documentation to review and the steps to follow:

  • Missing Learning tenant
    KBA 3609059: This would be potentially observed during the implementation phase. Learning instance provisioning is now a self-service tool in SAP for Me. Apart from BizX, no other modules are auto provisioned.

  • Incorrect Learning URL loaded
    KBA 2705153: Check whether the Learning button/link being selected is customized, as it can contain an incomplete/incorrect/outdated Learning URL. The correct URL can be found in BizX via the standard Learning navigation options or in SAP for Me by opening the system and checking the “Main URL”. 

  • Unauthorized assertion consumer service: https://******.lms.hr.cloud.sap/learning/saml/SSO@sf_idp_metadata
    KBA 3605368: This is caused by incorrect configuration in Authorized SP Assertion Consumer Service Settings. In this case, the expected values are: Assertion Consumer Service > https://domain.lms.hr.cloud.sap/learning/saml/SSO  | Logout Url > https://domain.lms.hr.cloud.sap/learning/saml/SingleLogout.

  • “Unable to retrieve idp credential@sf_idp_metadata” error when trying to access Learning
    KBA 3589551: Please, ask the user to clear the browser’s cache and cookies to resolve it (KBA 2310570).

  • Locked Learning Administrator sees "Learning Administration" option in “Dashboard” menu for locked Learning Administrator
    KBA 3747270: Locking the Learning Administrator doesn’t remove the “Learning Administration" option from the “Dashboard” menu. If the button is clicked, a validation error happens. Either unlock the Learning Administrator account or remove the “Related User” account. 

  • "You do not have the appropriate permissions to access the Learning module" error or being logged out when trying to access Learning
    KBA 2461828: It could be related to a user ID mismatch between BizX and Learning. Make sure to confirm the user has the proper Learning permissions in BizX via KBA 2489829.

  • "The requested operation is not available" error when trying to access Learning
    KBA 2590739: Authorized SP Assertion Consumer Service Settings or “baseURL” and/or “metadataProviders.IDP1.value” are incorrectly configured. To review the Authorized SP Assertion Consumer Service Settings, please check KBA 3605368. To review the “baseURL” and “metadataProviders.IDP1.value” settings, please open a case (“LOD-SF-LMS-INT” component), as these configurations are in Learning Administration > System Administration > Configuration > System Configuration > BIZX. 

  • Learning Administration/User UI doesn’t load
    KBA 3169042: “successFactorsCompanyID” and/or “metadataProviders.IDP1.value” have an incorrect value. It’ll require a case to analyze and adjust it (“LOD-SF-LMS-INT” component), as these configurations are in Learning Administration > System Administration > Configuration > System Configuration > BIZX. 

  • "company is not valid: companyID@sf_init" error when trying to access Learning Administration after refresh activity
    KBA 3577236: This can happen after a refresh. To correct it by yourself, Learning can’t be connected to BizX and Learning native login is required. Otherwise, this needs a case (“LOD-SF-LMS-INT” component), so SAP Support can adjust it. 

Details about the implementation process can be found here: KBA 3209088. SAP Partners can also refer to the SAP SuccessFactors Learning Integration Configuration Guide. 

If you know any other scenario that should be listed above, please leave a comment so we can update the content.

entry.title: Re: Runtime error PERFORM_INDEX_0 when running payroll si...
entry.content:
Hi All, 

In my scenario the costumer reach the maximum number of payroll custom functions.

I used a solution based on SAP note 1324745 - PE04: Namespace for country/customer functions is used up - SAP for Me

The new international standard subroutine FUXCALL is created as a solution. In future, each function that has this subroutine as 'Name of user-defined form routine' in transaction PE04 (Maintain Functions and Operations) is assigned the value 170 (= hex AA) during schema generation.
Within FUXCALL, the subroutine FUXCALL_NATIO is called for countries and the subroutine FUXCALL_CUST is called for customers. There, you can navigate to the actual code for your new function using a statement CASE AS-FUNCO. (for this, use the name for the form routine that you would actually have entered in transaction PE04 instead of FUXCALL).

In PE04 transaction use a Self-Defined name as FUXCALL instead of Standard Name.

This solution means that the system will use index AA in table T52A0 instead of creating a new index.

Regards.

entry.title: Re: How to force a re-calculation of a formula (pricing)?
entry.content:
This is a very old post with a slightly different question, but it's the only one that comes up as one of the top hits on Google when searching why pricing is not triggered when a header-level field is changed. And the previously linked SCN thread no longer exists. So I thought I'd add more info here. (This is valid in ECC and S/4HANA that is NOT Public Cloud.)

SAP Note 26115 has a list of fields that trigger re-pricing based on the pricing type. You may be surprised that list of fields is actually very short. For example, Order Reason (VBAK-AUGRU) update does not trigger re-pricing. This probably makes sense to someone at SAP but not to you when this field is included in pricing communication structure and you have a request to write up a requirement routine that involves the reason code. The routine will work just once, when you enter the order reason and then enter the line item. And you would think "mission accomplished" but then will be in for a nasty surprise when you change the order reason and... nothing happens, pricing of the item stays the same. (Again, makes sense for some, doesn't make life easier for others.)

The note hints at using userexit_new_pricing_vbkd in MV45AFZB and as bizarre as it is (we aren't changing any fields in VBKD!), it actually works if you put something like this there:

 IF *vbak-augru <> vbak-augru.
    new_pricing = 'C'.
 ENDIF.


If you don't do it (or find another way to force re-pricing), then any pricing routines will not work correctly if the field in question changes, unless it's one of the lucky ones mentioned in the note. 

I'm not sure if there is a better way for this in 2026 using BAdI or something. Unfortunately, there is no easily discoverable and openly available information for ABAPers like "before you did X, now you can do Y". Barely a mentioning of BAdIs in documentation, the rest we are somehow expected to figure out, I guess. ¯\_(ツ)_/¯

Cheers!

🚀 Participe do Nosso Próximo Webinar: Relatórios do SAP Fieldglass: recursos e inteligência de última geração

Você está pronto para dar o próximo passo na análise e gestão da força de trabalho?

Temos o prazer de convidá-lo para o nosso próximo webinar: Relatórios do SAP Fieldglass: recursos e inteligência de última geração, onde vamos explorar as mais recentes inovações em analytics e relatórios, com foco em benchmarking e sustentabilidade.

💡 O que você vai aprender

Cristina Barroso é Consultora de Valor de Soluções, especializada em soluções SAP Fieldglass. Nesse papel, atua em estreita colaboração com clientes e equipes multifuncionais, impulsionando resultados de negócios mensuráveis e apoiando a execução das estratégias dos clientes da SAP.

Como especialista reconhecida, Cristina apoia organizações na transformação da gestão da força de trabalho contingente e na aquisição de serviços, por meio da adoção de soluções tecnológicas inovadoras.

Baseada no SAP Labs em São Leopoldo, atua na condução de iniciativas de implementação e consultoria, trabalhando em parceria com clientes para otimizar a gestão da força de trabalho externa e os processos de aquisição de serviços.

entry.title: Re: Hidden Crisis: Ageing Infrastructure, Reactive O&M, and the Data Problem No One Is Solving
entry.content:
Very relevant perspective. Thank you for sharing.

One point I would add: trusted master data also depends on trusted operational data capture at the point of execution.

 

In asset- and field-intensive environments, many data-quality issues originate where work actually happens: inspections, work orders, notifications, spare-parts usage, forms, photos, measurements and technician feedback.

 

That is why the execution layer matters so much. Clean master data, SAP EAM/APM processes and AI-ready operational data only scale if field teams can capture high-quality information in a simple, reliable and adoption-friendly way.

 

From my perspective, this is where SAP-native execution apps can strongly complement the MDM foundation: by closing the gap between asset strategy, maintenance planning and real work performed in the field.

entry.title: Re: GIS and SAP Integration – critical for Oil and Gas Companies
entry.content:
Thanks you. Very relevant perspective, especially for linear assets and pipeline operations.

 

From my point of view, the GIS/SAP integration becomes even more powerful when it is connected to a strong mobile execution layer in the field.

GIS helps locate the asset precisely. SAP structures the notification, work order and maintenance process. But the critical moment is still the actual execution: technician input, photos, forms, measurements, spare-parts usage, offline work and confirmation quality.

In asset- and field-intensive environments, this is often where operational data quality is either created or lost.

That is why I see a strong complementarity between GIS-enabled SAP workflows and SAP-native execution apps: turning precise location intelligence into reliable field execution and high-quality operational data inside the SAP landscape.

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA PP Certification – What is the new system...
entry.content:
Hi Paul, 

Here my answers: 

1. it is 100% system-based

2. It is a concrete business scenario that must be implemented step by step in the system. But there are also additional requirements that are not directly formulated as questions.

3. It clearly helps save time if you know which apps exist and what they are used for. However, it is nowhere stated that working in the GUI is forbidden; rather, in some cases, you also have to do that.

4. It all depends on your experience, I didn't have enough time on the first try. I recommend that after starting, you first use the 24 hours to thoroughly study the task and come up with a concrete approach. Then set-up the practice system.

5. Similar to my previous answer. But without experience, in my opinion, it is not possible to solve everything, even with AI or Google.

6. I estimate the share in the scenario is 1/3 each for Customizing / Masterdata Creation / Execution.

I consider myself experienced and work as a system administrator PP, but I narrowly failed twice with 62%. However, I completed all the tasks and honestly do not understand exactly where the mistakes are, and I have also asked the support specific questions about this (of course not to get the exact solutions), but unfortunately received no helpful answers. In my opinion, there are also ambiguities in the scenario, but since I have received no answers, I cannot confirm this.

Really a helpful tip, take your time when executing, because when working with multiple logins it quickly happens that you are logged in incorrectly or the sometimes very similar names (WC, Routing, Material, etc.) quickly lead to avoidable mistakes.

I wish you good luck and don't forget to tell me how you did it. And of course, I would be happy about a like and "accepted solution" of this answer.

Sincerely Jan

entry.title: Re: Clarification on SAP Datasphere Capacity Units (CU) U...
entry.content:
Hi there, well you will not be happy with the answers :). 

CU Overage / CAP 250 CUs Per day / CAP 1300 CUs per Month
The system will not prevent operations from continuing, allowing you to exceed your allocated CUs to maintain business continuity. Overages will be tracked and Customer Success Partner (CSP) should contact you within a month to discuss further steps.

No notification is possible as of now via DSP GUI. If you have access to CLI, you can setup cron-job, but CLI is a complete different story 🙂

BTP allows Spaces/Quotas. But this is about it. DSP, nor SAP for Me allows anything even near. Architecture is not to disturb operations ever. Logical, you do not want your system blocked when being used 🙂

SAP for Me: AP for Me by navigating to Finance & Legal → Consumption. (https://me.sap.com/financelegal/Consumption).  Have you checked already in your avl. toolset? 

DW Administrator Role provides all the necessary authorizations. 

 


https://me.sap.com/notes/3507826

https://me.sap.com/notes/3650515

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3583153

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_DATASPHERE/9f804b8efa8043539289f42f372c4862/ba3d05baac854171914c09d64bed7202.html

 

Don't forget to praise with via accepting and liking answers. 
Cheers, Martin

So we have SAP Ariba Invoicing (SAP BTP CIM) for past 2+ years on our DEV instance gathering dust. But we heard that SAP had improved their software and so we did a little bit of testing on our instance last week and it seems to be better.  

So there now there seems to be 3 options?
SAP Ariba Invoice Management (part of SAP Ariba solution suite)
Customer-managed SAP Ariba Invoicing (that we have - the "original" SAP BTP CIM)
SAP-managed SAP Ariba Invoicing aka next-gen SAP Ariba Invoicing

entry.title: Re: Demical are prohibited in Internal Order
entry.content: The error “Decimals are prohibited in Internal Order” is triggered because the user is entering a comma (,) in the order number field, which SAP interprets as a decimal due to their SU01 settings. The solution is simply to enter the internal order number without any decimal or thousands separator. If the internal order really is 2878282, they should type it without a comma: 2878282.

entry.title: Information Design Tool does not connect to server 4.3 SP05 patch 5
entry.content:
Hello guys
I am having a new problem with upgrading SAP Business Objects Enterprise from version 4.3 SP04 patch 8 to 4.3 SP05 patch 5.

Information Design Tool (obviously updated to 4.3 SP05 patch 5) no longer connects to the updated system:
As soon as I enter the parameters to open the session (system, username and password), I get this error:

Error:
Failed to log on host "@XXXXX:6400", user "Administrator", Could not reach CMS '@XXXXX:6400'. Specify the correct host and port and check for network issues, please refer to SAP Knowledge Base Article 2151044 on the SAP Support Portal. (FWM 20030)

Cause of Error
Could not reach CMS '@XXXXX:6400'. Specify the correct host and port and check for network issues, please refer to SAP Knowledge Base Article 2151044 on the SAP Support Portal. (FWM 20030)

Cause of Error
Server XXXXX:6400 not found or server may be down (FWM 01003)

Consider a few things:
- Obviously, the system is up & running (I can logon in the CMC/BI Launchpad and refresh reports with no issues);
- All processing servers have a statically assigned port and the telnet from the machine where IDT is installed to the server, works correctly on the ports of all processing servers (even the request port of the CMS).
- IDT installed on the same machine as the server (I know it's not recommended by SAP, but I did it anyway =D ), connects without problems.

At this point I wonder: is it possible that, for version 4.3 SP05 patch 5, there is some allow list to be filled in to allow access from IDT?
Or can you think of something else?

Thank you so much guys!
Best regards.
Andrea

entry.title: Re: Information Design Tool does not connect to server 4....

Everything seems fine now, but I'll run more tests on Monday.

Thanks everyone

entry.title: Adding New Attribute for Restart Conditions in Flexible Workflow
entry.content:
Dear All,

I have a requirement to introduce a new purchase order attribute (either at the header or item level) within the "Manage Conditions to Restart Flexible Workflow" configuration, specifically for handling purchase order amendments.

However, I have observed that the current configuration node does not support the addition of new attributes. Additionally, I was unable to identify any BAdI that facilitates extending or adding new attributes to this configuration.

While I have been able to influence the restart condition behavior using the BAdI MM_PUR_S4_CTR_WFL_RESTART, which allows customization based on existing specific attribute values, I am still unable to incorporate new attributes into the restart condition framework.

I would appreciate your guidance on whether there is any feasible approach or recommended enhancement to achieve this requirement.

Thank you in advance for your support.

Regards,
Suddy

entry.title: Re: FB05 transfer posting with clearing does not update W...
entry.content:
Why FB05 does not trigger WHT is because, clearing transactions (FB05 transfer posting with clearing) are not considered payment-relevant and this is the standard SAP behaviour. Because IRF is configured as “at payment,” the system only recalculates withholding tax during payment transactions (F110, F-53, FBZ2, FB05 payment variant). That is why WITH_ITEM is not updated and you see message 7Q332 – “Withholding tax information is not considered.”

No standard way exists to force WHT calculation during FB05 transfer posting with clearing. For Brazil localization, the recommendation is to use payment transactions (F110, F-53, FBZ2) when IRF is “at payment.”

If business requires WHT during clearing, it is a gap and SAP expects you to handle it either by redesigning the process (settle via payment) or by building a custom enhancement.

entry.title: Re: Adding New Attribute for Restart Conditions in Flexib...
entry.content:
Dear @Sudharmv,

In Public Cloud there is currently no standard configuration option to add a new customer-defined purchase order header or item attribute into the attribute list of Manage Conditions to Restart Flexible Workflow for Purchase Orders / SSCUI 103345. The configuration is based on SAP-delivered restart-relevant PO attributes

More details below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3053155

For purchase orders, SAP provides two standard control mechanisms:

  1. SSCUI 103345 Manage Conditions to Restart Flexible Workflow for Purchase Orders where the available PO attributes can be selected as restart-relevant
  2. BAdI MMPUR_PO_WORKFLOW_RESTART Define Conditions to Restart Workflow in Purchase Orders, which can be used to implement additional custom logic to decide whether the approval workflow should restart after a PO change

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/259a396e6bdb4d08b130049880a3920f.html

SAP confirms that the BAdI can be used to apply own custom logic and can override the restart behavior defined in the SSCUI. However this does not extend the SSCUI itself and does not add a new customer field as a selectable restart attribute in the configuration UI. Therefore if the business requirement is to make a new amendment-related field available directly as a configurable restart condition, this is not supported as a standard Public Cloud configuration extension

More details below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3736482

The recommended approach is to keep the delivered SSCUI restart attributes and implement the amendment-specific restart decision in MMPUR_PO_WORKFLOW_RESTART, provided the required amendment indicator or field value is available in the BAdI context or can be read using released Public Cloud extensibility. If the required field is not available through released extensibility the clean-core route is to raise a feature request / SAP Influence request for SAP to extend the restart-condition framework

More details below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2963059

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: How to schedule Monthly Physical Inventory Creation f...
entry.content:
SAP recommends separate scheduled jobs per indicator if you want different automation rules.  For example:

  • Job 1 → Cycle Counting, Indicator A, monthly
  • Job 2 → Cycle Counting, Indicator B, quarterly
  • Job 3 → Cycle Counting, Indicator C, yearly (or manual)

This keeps the automation aligned with your ABC classification.

entry.title: Re: Cyclic Count issue in MICN transaction
entry.content:
Why you see “Cyclic Count Breached” is because of the following:

  • MICN compares the planned count date (based on cycle counting interval + last count date) with the current date.
  • If the planned date has passed and no physical inventory document was created/posted, the system flags the material as breached (red).
  • That is why you see red dates — those items are overdue for counting.

The material creation date is not directly relevant. What matters is:

  • The last physical inventory document posting date (or initial assignment if none exists).
  • The interval defined for the cycle counting indicator.

How the interval works is the interval (e.g., 4 for “A”) is expressed in months. The system does not run on a fixed April–March calendar. Instead, it always calculates the next planned count date based on the last physical inventory document date (or initialization date if never counted).

Example:

  • Indicator A = 4 months.
  • Last PI document posted on 15 Jan 2026.
  • Next planned count date = 15 May 2026.
  • If you don’t count by then, MICN will show “breached.”

So, it is rolling, relative to the last count date, not tied to fiscal year or calendar year boundaries.  That is why you see mismatches if you expected April–March alignment.

Having said that the Best Practices are

  1. Run MICN regularly (monthly, weekly) to catch due items before they breach.
  2. Ensure initial PI documents are created for new materials — otherwise the system uses creation date as a baseline, which can look odd.
  3. If business wants strict April–March cycles, you’d need custom logic or manual scheduling, because SAP standard cycle counting is always relative to the last count.

entry.title: Re: Pros and Cons of implementing full MDG within Central...
entry.content:
Hi Imran,

Based on my experience, keeping MDG as a separate hub in a central finance scenario will increase your integration efforts for master data. Eg: If you create a business partner in MDG hub ( BP1 ) , you would have to distribute it to your source ERP and CFIN. Considering all the number ranges are same across all the systems. However most of the time its not the case, your source system will have a different number considering its legacy, while your MDG and CFIN might be same. Imagine having 10+ source system and 5-6 master data objects the integration efforts will be huge.

Keeping MDG within CFIN is a good option in the long run and it can scale. I don't think there are any best practices recommendations, however you can check out the CFIN central note to see if there are any suggestions.

Regards

Anirban

entry.title: Re: Crystal Reports 8 license doesn't allow subreport man...
entry.content:
Hi Andrew, CRAXDDRT was the embeddable Report Designer that Developers could add to their app to allow the user to open a Designer within their app and edit reports.

I started with CR in 1995 when CR 5 was just released and worked in OEM Support. But it's been so long I don't recall exactly now either...

But I do know that a Developer Keycode was required to enable RCAPI features, not sure now though if it was for both CRAXDRT and CRAXDDRT or just one of them...

In any event with the RCAPI keycode he won't be able to edit Reports, just able to set basic functions.

And installing Crystal Reports Designer created the registry key, under BusinessObject and look something like the latest one:

Computer\HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\SAP BusinessObjects\Suite XI 4.0\Crystal Reports\Keycodes\CR Dev

I could ask someone at SAP and ask them if anyone still has the old RCAPI license info, on the off chance some of the old guys still has it. I doubt though but a few I know do keep the old stuff ...

entry.title: How to generate alert in Alert Inbox with other language instead of English in SAP Solution Manager
entry.content:
The job SAP_ALERT_CALCULATION_ENGINE is scheduled in English by default.

Change the language of the released SAP_ALERT_CALCULATION_ENGINE job in transaction sm37 as below:

  1. Go to sm37 and use the following selection options:
    Job Name: SAP_ALERT_CALCULATION_ENGINE
    User Name: *
    Job Status: Released
  2. Click 'Change' from the 'Job' menu bar.
  3. Click 'Step' button then click 'Change' button.
    In the 'ABAP program' field, make sure the name is ACE_CALCULATION_CONTROLLER.
    In the 'Language' input field, change the language, e.g. JA - Japanese, or the language that you want to generate the alert in Alert Inbox.
  4. Save your changes and click on the 'Back' pushbutton to go back to the 'Change Job SAP_ALERT_CALCULATION_ENGINE' screen.
  5. Save your changes.
  6. Click button 'Apply and Activate' in the following path: 
    T-code: Solman_setup > application operations > system monitoring > step 6 setup monitoring.

Oppositely, if this job is scheduled in another language and now you want to get English alert, change the existing released job and schedule it in English.

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere & BDC - Is system readiness analysis r...
entry.content:
Hi Vishala,

Yes/No. 

No for Data volume, spaces definition, spaces architecture, ... 

Knowing the data volume is critical. On Projects everything changes, so exact value is not needed, but you have to know at least what to expect. Very important is Snapshots / Data Redundancy (can you always load data from source systems, or not? If not, where/how will you backup your Snapshots? - there are a few ways, like local tables, or sinking into Azure). Important also is the size of the data during calculations. Will you persist the data, or run ad-hoc for each user? Persisting is always the preferred method as you do not want to run into OoM (80% default space safety belt) 

No for Reports, Extraction, ... This can be done as you go. 

Reports from BO into SAC should be OK. There just get a common design based on the existing BO reports. You may need to tweak SAC to provide WebI functionality, but this can be done while doing the migration. Just define what will be common standard and what Story standard. 

Loading the data is also not needed to deepdive up front. Since you change the source to S/4 (PCE, or Public?) you may need to use the compatibility views, or just switch to CDS views loaded via Replication flows. 

Cheers, Martin
